phase_1.label: transition_static_to_wordpress
phase_1.rationale: Conservative staged rollout during WordPress transition: real HSTS protection with faster recovery window than one year if TLS or redirect misconfiguration occurs.

phase_2.label: wordpress_stable_production
phase_2.trigger: After WordPress production cutover, full smoke test, and >=30 days without TLS/redirect incidents
phase_2.rationale: Original audit approved full-year host-only HSTS with high confidence; deferred until operational uncertainty of migration ends.

ultra_conservative_alternative.tradeoff: Minimal browser pin during transition; weaker effective HSTS protection for returning visitors within the transition window.

immediate_full_year_alternative.note: Original audit recommendation; still technically safe for this site; project prefers staged approach per ADR 0018.

exact_recommended_action_phase_1: In .htaccess line ~103, replace the commented HSTS line with: Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=604800" and deploy. Do not activate max-age=31536000 until phase 2 unless project explicitly opts out of staging (ADR 0018).
validation_steps_phase_1: curl -sS -D - -o /dev/null https://caminodeldharma.org/ | grep -i strict-transport-security  → exactly max-age=604800
Full navigation smoke test of all 13 pages over HTTPS
Confirm certificate expiry monitoring exists (cert valid to 2026-10-12)
validation_steps_phase_2: Same curl matrix as phase 1 with expected max-age=31536000
>=30 days stable production since WordPress cutover
rollback_constraints: Never roll back by simply removing the header: serve Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=0" over valid HTTPS
Effective pin duration equals active max-age (604800 in phase 1; up to 31536000 in phase 2)
Rollback requires working TLS; certificate renewal must be monitored
residual_risks: Certificate renewal history could not be verified via CT logs (crt.sh 502, EVID-0030)
Phase 1 provides shorter HSTS protection than one-year pin until phase 2 is deployed
